title: 2-in-1 metabolite and protein extraction method for plant multi-omics
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f3912","text":"This protocol is a method for extracting metabolites and proteins from the same plant samples, and is the protocol used in manuscript entitled \"Lyophilization Improved Efficiency in 2-in-1 Metabolite and Protein Extraction for Plant Multi-Omics\",  https://doi.org/10.1002/pmic.70068","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":248,"length":34}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"0E0A119BAF4F11F098610A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://doi.org/10.1002/pmic.70068"}}}}

steps: Sample collection Collect plant material using a humidity chamber, weigh 150 mg for each 2 mL screw cap tube (Cole-Parmer, Vernon Hills, IL, USA), add a 6 mm stainless steel grinding ball (Cole-Parmer, Vernon Hills, IL, USA). Put tubes in liquid nitrogen immediately. Liquid nitrogen is extremely cold and can be hazardous if not handled properly.

Sample homogenizes For each tube, add a 6 mm stainless steel grinding ball (Cole-Parmer, Vernon Hil ls, IL, USA). Freeze the tube with liquid nitrogen, homogenize with homogenizer (HG-400, Cole-Parmer, Vernon Hills, IL, USA) for 1000 rpm, °C, 2 minutes . Add liquid nitrogen to the clamp to remain tubes cold. Cole-Parmer SamplePrep HG-400 MiniG® Homogenizer (homogenizer, Cole-Parmer, EW-04500-08, https://www.coleparmer.com/i/cole-parmer-sampleprep-hg-400-minig-homogenizer-2-to-50-ml-115-230-vac-50-60hz/0450008 )
Sample vacuum dried Vacuum drying samples with Savant SPD1010 SpeedVac Concentrator System (Thermo Scientific, Asheville, NC, USA). Savant SPD1010 SpeedVac Concentrator System (Savant, SPD1010)

Add 800 µL pre-cooled (-20 °C) metabolite extraction buffer I (acetonitrile: isopropanol: water, 3:3:2) with 0.1 mM PMSF to the tube. Remove the bead in the tube with magnetic sticker. Vortex 30 seconds, sonicate in ice water for 5 min, centrifuge 15000 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes . Phenylmethylsulfonyl fluoride (PMSF) is a serine protease inhibitor. It is highly reactive and hazardous, requiring careful handling. Use Gloves, Eye protection, Lab coat, and Respirator.

Add internal standards For each sample tube, add 3 internal standards: 10 µL of 100 µM lidocaine (metabolomics, positive mode) 10 µL of 100 µM 10-camphorsulfonic acid (10-CA, metabolomics, negative mode) 4 µL of 1 µg/µL Bovine Serum Albumin Fraction V (BSA, proteomics)
Pipetting supernatant to a new 1.5 mL centrifuge tube A. The 1.5 mL supernatant tube A should be kept in a -20 °C freezer.

Add 800 µL pre-cooled (-20 °C) metabolite extraction buffer II (acetonitrile: water, 1:1) with 0.1 mM PMSF to the pellet. Vortex 30 seconds, sonicate in ice water for 5 min, centrifuge 15000 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes . Phenylmethylsulfonyl fluoride (PMSF) is a serine protease inhibitor. It is highly reactive and hazardous, requiring careful handling. Use Gloves, Eye protection, Lab coat, and Respirator.
Pipetting supernatant to a new 1.5 mL centrifuge tube B. The 1.5 mL supernatant tube B should be kept in a -20 °C freezer.

Add 800 µL pre-cooled (-20 °C) metabolite extraction buffer III (80% methanol) with 0.1 mM PMSF to the pellet. Vortex 30 seconds, sonicate in ice water for 5 min, centrifuge 15000 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes . Phenylmethylsulfonyl fluoride (PMSF) is a serine protease inhibitor. It is highly reactive and hazardous, requiring careful handling. Use Gloves, Eye protection, Lab coat, and Respirator.
Pipetting supernatant to a new 1.5 mL centrifuge tube C. The remaining residue will be used in protein extraction (step 13)

Vacuum dry the three collected tubes, combine extracts from the same sample together after 1 h of vacuum drying. Pipet the sample in B, C tubes to A tube, so that all metabolites will be transferred and combined in one tube for drying down.
Vacuum dry metabolite extraction residue (from step 10) for 5 min to evaporate methanol remaining.

Add 200 µL protei n Lysis solubilization solution (7 M urea, 2 M thiourea, 4% CHAPS, 10 mM DTT and 0.1 mM PMSF). Phenylmethylsulfonyl fluoride (PMSF) is a serine protease inhibitor. It is highly reactive and hazardous, requiring careful handling. Use Gloves, Eye protection, Lab coat, and Respirator.
Vortex in cold room (4 °C) for 30 min, sonicate in ice water for 5 min, centrifuge 15000 x g, 4 °C, 15 minutes .
Collect the supernatant, this is the protein solvent.
Once dried down, the residue is the metabolite section.
creator: Qijie Guan
